source_decisions.primary_interface: One Signal thread with Torben as the user-facing operator.
source_decisions.runtime_boundary: Torben owns scheduling, Signal delivery, dedupe, action handles, and reply routing. Magnus owns GTM source research, ranking, drafting engines, and approval-package evidence.
source_decisions.model_routing: Use Grok/xAI for Magnus GTM research, X Search, social review, and GTM drafting. Use OpenAI/Codex only when explicitly generating or editing long-form content or code.
source_decisions.cron_policy: Crons wake bounded LLM workflows. Static code may collect evidence, cap budget, validate outputs, and enforce no-mutation boundaries, but must not be the decision maker for GTM signal.
source_decisions.noise_policy: Successful no-op maintenance is silent. Signal only receives actionable findings, approval-ready drafts, direct reply opportunities, or failures that need operator attention.
source_decisions.public_action_policy: Public posting, replies, likes, reposts, follows, DMs, scheduling, and browser automation remain blocked until explicit approval and a public-action guard path exists.

definition_of_done: Torben reliably surfaces timely, deduped, link-backed GTM signal and approval-ready content packages through one Signal thread, while Magnus continuously improves topic selection, content quality, reply opportunities, and timing from performance evidence without unattended public mutations.

gtm_intelligence_loop.purpose: Find what Eric should know and act on before the topic is stale.
sources: arXiv papers on security, AI security, agents, MCP, evals, observability, and AI engineering leverage
security news sources including THN-style vulnerabilities, TTPs, hardening, threat hunting, and tool releases
YC, HN, GitHub, vendor research, and builder discourse
Grok/X Search discourse for what practitioners are arguing about or amplifying
EA newsletter extraction artifacts from security, AI, founder, and tool newsletters
outputs: 2-3 sentence summary for each selected item
why this matters to Eric's brand, businesses, or operating leverage
recommended route: learn, monitor, short post, reply, thread, or longform
visual direction when the topic is content-worthy
acceptance: No duplicate finding should re-alert unless there is a materially new angle.
X discourse can shape angle and timing but must not be the only factual source for strong claims.
If the source refresh fails, Torben surfaces one actionable failure instead of a normal brief.

gtm_content_creation_loop.purpose: Turn accepted intelligence into approval-ready founder-led content.
outputs: long-form article briefs and full drafts
image, diagram, icon, and visual asset prompts
platform-specific context explaining why the draft should exist
gtm_content_creation_loop.voice: Founder voice paired with Magnus: sharp, direct, crass when useful, high-conviction, memorable, and practical without generic AI slop.
acceptance: Every package includes the source evidence, thesis, target audience, visual plan, approval options, and public_action_status=blocked_until_approval.
No package can be posted or scheduled by cron.

gtm_optimization_engagement_loop.purpose: Use audience behavior and live discourse to improve what Magnus finds and drafts next.
inputs: approval, hold, revise, and rejection outcomes
outputs: timely reply recommendations with exact copy and context
topic, hook, format, and timing recommendations
negative memory for rejected or stale topics
acceptance: Near-real-time response discovery uses deterministic prefilters plus Grok on only the highest-potential candidates.
Token use is capped by budget and relevance thresholds.
Recommendations are approval-gated and never become unattended replies.

implementation_gates.name: Baseline and runtime ownership
current_evidence: Torben gateway is running and owns the Signal thread.
Magnus gateway is not running, so Magnus cron jobs are not the reliable scheduler.
Torben and Magnus xai-oauth profile auth are available for Grok/xAI paths.
Latest Torben GTM adapter artifact suppressed duplicate findings instead of sending repeat chat noise.

implementation_gates.name: Torben-owned GTM radar source refresh
implementation_gates.change: Modify torben_gtm_radar.py so each non-preview Torben run refreshes the Magnus radar before adapting findings to Signal.
acceptance: Torben no longer depends on a separate running Magnus gateway for the GTM radar.
Refresh uses the Magnus profile home and xAI/Grok-capable environment.
Successful no-new-finding runs remain silent.
Refresh failure wakes Torben with a failure message and public_actions_taken=0.
Preview mode does not mutate Magnus seen ledgers, Torben action ledger, or delivered-finding state.

implementation_gates.name: Source breadth and newsletter bridge
implementation_gates.change: Feed EA newsletter extraction artifacts and additional security/AI/GitHub sources into the GTM radar.
acceptance: Previous-day newsletter stories and tools can become GTM findings with links and dedupe keys.
arXiv scan runs often enough to teach Eric and feed content, not just twice weekly.
Security, security+AI, and AI-engineering leverage pillars are all represented.

implementation_gates.name: Timely engagement radar
implementation_gates.change: Run a bounded X/LinkedIn engagement scout through Torben with Grok drafting only for high-scoring candidates.
acceptance: Mention/reply opportunities are checked frequently enough to matter.
Broad watchlist searches are batched to avoid token and API burn.
Signal receives only the best reply opportunities with context, exact draft, and approval options.
No public reply is sent from cron.

implementation_gates.name: Approval and distribution package
implementation_gates.change: Connect approved content packages to X/LinkedIn/Postiz or browser automation behind explicit approval.
acceptance: Approval commands resolve to the original evidence and exact draft.
Publishing paths record audit metadata and platform response.
Scheduling remains blocked until approval and guard checks pass.

implementation_gates.name: Performance optimization loop
implementation_gates.change: Feed engagement outcomes back into topic selection, hooks, cadence, and watchlists.
acceptance: Accepted, rejected, revised, and high-performing drafts change future ranking.
Repeated weak angles are suppressed.
Torben can explain what changed and why.

live_canaries: Torben preview run: does not refresh or mutate unless TORBEN_GTM_REFRESH_MAGNUS_PREVIEW=1.
Magnus refresh smoke: produces JSON, public_actions_taken=0, external_mutations=0.
Torben adapter latest artifact: wakeAgent=false when all findings are duplicate, with source_refresh metadata after live refresh.

open_boundaries: Do not re-enable legacy Magnus OpenClaw jobs.
Do not turn response discovery into unattended public replies.
Do not burn Grok tokens on low-scoring broad scans.
Do not send normal cron success reports into Signal.
Do not treat browser automation as ready for public posting until approval, audit, and rollback paths exist.
